memo_markdown:
## 1. Recommendation

**PROCEED WITH CONDITIONS.** The committee recommends proceeding, subject to conditions. Base-case equity IRR is 17.7 % [E:E-VAL-IRR] against a minimum DSCR of 1.853 × [E:E-DBT-MIN-DSCR]. Levelised cost of energy is 70.13 EUR/MWh [E:E-VAL-LCOE], framing the merchant downside.

## 2. Asset & Resource

Installed capacity 13.9 [E:E-INP-MW] across 7 [E:E-INP-N-UNITS] turbines.

P50 annual energy is 41.33 GWh [E:E-ENE-P50] at a net capacity factor of 33.94 % [E:E-ENE-NET-CF]; the P90 estimate is 36.31 GWh [E:E-ENE-P90].

## 3. Revenue & Market

Year-one revenue is 3.692 M€ [E:E-REV-Y1] with a wind capture rate of 100.00 % [E:E-REV-CAPTURE].

## 4. Financial Structure

| Metric | Value | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Average DSCR | 1.855 × | [E:E-DBT-AVG-DSCR] |
| Sculpted debt capacity | 23,231,293.0 EUR | [E:E-DBT-CAPACITY] |
| Senior debt drawn | 16,263,000.0 EUR | [E:E-DBT-DRAWN] |
| DSRA funded at close | 583,299.3 EUR | [E:E-DBT-DSRA] |
| Gearing achieved | 0.75 | [E:E-DBT-GEARING] |
| LLCR | 1.891 × | [E:E-DBT-LLCR] |
| Minimum DSCR | 1.853 × | [E:E-DBT-MIN-DSCR] |
| PLCR | 2.229 × | [E:E-DBT-PLCR] |
| Net capacity factor | 0.3394 | [E:E-ENE-NET-CF] |
| P50 annual energy | 41.33 GWh | [E:E-ENE-P50] |
| P75 annual energy | 38.69 GWh | [E:E-ENE-P75] |
| P90 annual energy | 36.31 GWh | [E:E-ENE-P90] |
| 1-year P90 energy (incl. inter-annual) | 35.39 GWh | [E:E-ENE-P90-1YR] |
| Combined uncertainty sigma | 0.09487 | [E:E-ENE-SIGMA] |
| Wind capture rate | 1.0 | [E:E-REV-CAPTURE] |
| CFADS, year 1 | 2,413,666.8 EUR | [E:E-REV-CFADS-Y1] |
| EBITDA, year 1 | 2,658,178.3 EUR | [E:E-REV-EBITDA-Y1] |
| Market revenue, year 1 | 3,692,119.5 EUR | [E:E-REV-MARKET-Y1] |
| Opex, year 1 | 1,033,941.2 EUR | [E:E-REV-OPEX-Y1] |
| EEG premium revenue, year 1 | 0 EUR | [E:E-REV-PREMIUM-Y1] |
| Revenue, year 1 | 3,692,119.5 EUR | [E:E-REV-Y1] |
| Equity invested | 6,004,299.3 EUR | [E:E-VAL-EQUITY] |
| Equity IRR | 0.177 | [E:E-VAL-IRR] |
| LCOE (nominal) | 70.13 EUR/MWh | [E:E-VAL-LCOE] |
| NPV @ WACC (unlevered, after tax) | 4,768,565.2 EUR | [E:E-VAL-NPV] |
| Minimum DSCR at P90 energy | 1.529 × | [E:E-VAL-P90-DSCR] |
| Equity payback year | 6 | [E:E-VAL-PAYBACK] |

## 5. Risks & Mitigants

- **[concern]** Gearing is at the cap — debt capacity is leverage-limited.
- **[concern]** Assumed availability may be optimistic for the fleet age — O&M reserve advised.
- **[concern]** Resource uncertainty is wide (atlas-based estimate); P90 sits well below P50.
- **[concern]** Gearbox-era turbine technology carries elevated spares and reliability risk.
- **[concern]** Merchant exposure after the EEG support window is unhedged.
- **[concern]** A merchant tail follows the end of EEG support — a valuation swing factor.

## 6. Sensitivities

Under P90 energy the minimum DSCR moves to 1.529 × [E:E-VAL-P90-DSCR], the binding downside for the debt sizing.

## 7. Conditions Precedent

- Confirm the gearing cap is acceptable to the credit committee.
- Fund an availability/O&M reserve sized to the resource critic's downside.
- Obtain an independent (metered or Path-A) resource assessment before close.
- Secure a long-term full-service O&M and spares agreement for the fleet.
- Evaluate a PPA or hedge to cap merchant exposure after the support window.
- Reserve for the merchant-tail years or shorten the debt tenor accordingly.

## Appendix: Assumptions

WACC 5.8 % [E:E-ASM-WACC]; equity hurdle 8 % [E:E-ASM-HURDLE]; senior rate 3 % [E:E-ASM-RATE].

Rule-based memo: claims and prose are derived deterministically from the evidence store — no LLM was used. Figures trace to the citation seals.

claims.statement: Net capacity factor lands at 33.9% after farm losses, a credible P50 for this site class.
Assumed energy availability of 97% is optimistic for an older fleet and warrants an O&M reserve.
Combined resource uncertainty of sigma 0.095 pulls P90 well below P50; a Path-B (atlas) estimate carries wider error than metered data.
Turbine type E82 (commissioned 2002) is a gearbox-era machine — technology and spares risk is elevated.
Wind capture rate of 100% of baseload reflects the cannibalisation discount wind earns in high-wind hours.
The project runs fully merchant — no EEG premium floor, so cash flows track the wholesale curve one-for-one.
The §51 rule voids the premium only in negative streaks of 8760+ hours, a moderate exposure for this vintage.
Support ends at year 20 while the asset runs to year 25; the merchant tail is a real valuation swing factor.
Minimum DSCR of 1.85x sits 0.65x above the 1.20x covenant, giving real headroom.
Under P90 energy the minimum DSCR falls to 1.53x — coverage survives the resource downside.
Gearing reaches 75% — the cap binds, so debt capacity is leverage-limited, not cash-limited.
LLCR of 1.89x indicates loan-life coverage with comfortable margin.

rebuttals.statement: Credit view: the 1.85x coverage headroom absorbs this resource concern within the base-case sculpt; it is a pricing input, not a dealbreaker.
